3d_sdf_capped_cone.description: An SDF function for a capped cone of adjustable base and top radiuses.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the capped cone.

height.description: The Z-up height of the capped cone from its base.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

r1.description: The radius of the capped cone’s base.

r2.description: The radius of the capped cone’s top.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_capped_cone_2_points.description: An SDF function for a capped cone defined by the positions of its base and top. The base and top have adjustable radiuses.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

position_base.description: The position of the capped cone’s base.

position_top.description: The position of the capped cone’s top.

radius_base.description: The radius of the capped cone’s base.

radius_top.description: The radius of the capped cone’s top.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_capped_torus.description: An SDF function for a capped torus, where the sweeping of the minor circle along a major circle can be capped at an angle. Both circles have adjustable radiuses.

angle.description: The central angle, in turns, defining the trimming arc of the major circle along which the minor circle will not be swept.

angle_offset.description: The offset, along the major radius, of the trimming arc along which the minor circle will not be swept.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the capped torus.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

radius_major.description: The radius of the major circle along which the minor circle is swept to form the surface of the torus.

radius_minor.description: The radius of the minor circle being swept along the major circle to form the surface of the torus.

symmetric.description: Controls whether the trimming arc should be drawn in one or two directions.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_capsule.description: An SDF function for a capsule of adjustable length and radius. The capsule is the result of bridging two spheres.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the capsule.

end.description: The position of the end sphere.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

radius.description: The radius of both the start and end spheres.

start.description: The position of the start sphere.

start_end_at_tip.description: Controls whether the Start and End positions should be at the extremities of the spheres. I.e., controls if the height of the capsule should include the radius of the spheres.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_cone.description: An SDF function for a cone of adjustable height, radius and position.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the cone.

height.description: The Z-up height of the cone’s apex from its base.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

radius.description: The radius of the cone’s base.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_cube.description: An SDF function for a cube, with adjustable XYZ size and rounding of edges.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the cube.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

pivot_position.description: The world space position of the local pivot of the cube, where (0, 0, 0) places the pivot at the center of the cube.

rounding.description: The radius of the rounded arcs applied to the cube’s edges. Note: hard edges may appear where the rounding radiuses intersect.

size.description: The size of the cube on X, Y and Z.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_cylinder.description: An SDF function for a cylinder of adjustable height, radius and rounding of edges.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the cylinder.

height.description: The Z-up height of the cylinder from its base.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

pivot_position.description: The world space position of the local pivot of the cylinder, where (0, 0, 0) places the pivot at the center of the cylinder.

rounding.description: The radius of the rounded arcs applied to the cylinder’s edges. Note: hard edges may appear where the rounding radiuses intersect.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_cylinder_2_points.description: An SDF function for a cylinder of adjustable radius defined by the positions of its start and end discs.

end.description: The position of the cylinder’s end disc.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

start.description: The position of the cylinder’s start disc.

3d_sdf_ellipsoid.description: An SDF function for an ellipsoid, which is a rounded shape of adjustable tridimensional radius.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the ellipsoid.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

radius.description: The radius of the ellipsoid in X, Y and Z.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

3d_sdf_elongated_cylinder.description: An SDF function for an elongated cylinder of adjustable length, radius and rounding of edges. The elongated cylinder is the result of bridging two cylinders.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the elongated cylinder.

elong_width.description: The distance along which the start cylinder is elongated. I.e. the distance between the centers of the start and end cylinders.

height.description: The Z-up height of both the start and end cylinders from their base.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

radius.description: The radius of both the start and end cylinders.

rounding.description: The radius of the rounded arcs applied to the elongated cylinder’s edges. Note: hard edges may appear where the rounding radiuses intersect.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_ground_plane.description: An SDF function for an infinite ground plane, with adjustable height.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_helix.description: An SDF function for an approximation of a helix, which is a shape formed by sweeping a circle along a curve winding along an upward curve around an axis. Note: Since this SDF function is an approximation, artefacts might appear when rendering it.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the helix.

height.description: The Z-up height of the helix.

major_radius.description: The distance of the winding curve from the axis.

minor_radius.description: The radius of the circle being swept along the curve to form the surface of the helix.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

windings.description: The number of times the curve fully winds around the axis in steps of 0.5. I.e., how many times the helix will revolve within a height of 0.5.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_hexagonal_prism.description: An SDF function for a 6-sided prism of adjustable height, radius and rounding of edges.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the hexagonal prism.

height.description: The Z-up height of the hexagonal prism from its base.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

radius.description: The radius of the hexagonal prism.

rounding.description: The radius of the rounded arcs applied to the hexagonal prism’s edges. Note: hard edges may appear where the rounding radiuses intersect.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_infinite_plane.description: An SDF function for an infinite plane of adjustable orientation and position.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the plane, as a distance from the world origin along the plane’s normal.

normal.description: The world space normal vector of the infinite plane, which controls its orientation. The vector is normalized.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_op_intersection.description: Returns the volume common to two SDF shapes, effectively the volume created where two shapes overlap.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_op_intersection_smooth.description: Returns the volume common to two SDF shapes, effectively the volume created where two shapes overlap, with adjustable smoothing of the edges of their intersection.

smoothness.description: The smoothness of the edges at the intersection of the two SDF shapes. Note: hard edges may appear where the smoothing radiuses intersect.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_op_intersection_surface.description: Returns the surface of the portion of a base SDF shape that is intersected by another SDF shape, with adjustable thickness.

sdf_1.description: The SDF shape that the resulting surface is based on.

sdf_2.description: The SDF shape intersecting the base SDF shape.

thickness.description: The thickness of the resulting surface.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_op_morph.description: Returns the linear interpolation between a base SDF shape and a target SDF shape according to an adjustable mix factor.

mix_factor.description: The mix factor used to morph the input shapes, where 0 is the base shape, and 1 the target shape.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_op_repeat_mirror.description: Mirrors and duplicates an SDF shape any number of times at a regular spacing in the X, Y and Z positive or negative axes. Every time this operator repeats a shape, it also mirrors it. This visually results in an alternating between the original orientation of the shape and a flipped copy.

amount_neg.description: The amount of duplications along the negative X, Y, Z axes.

amount_pos.description: The amount of duplications along the negative X, Y, Z axes.

origin_position.description: Defines the center of the SDF shape that will be duplicated. The origin position is visualized by the cubic helper’s center position.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

spacing.description: The world space between each duplicate. The spacing is visualized by a cubic helper, which size is the space between duplicates in the X, Y and Z directions. The spacing starts at the Origin position and is increased symmetrically from it.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_op_rounding.description: Expands an SDF shape, inflating it and smoothing its hard edges.

radius.description: The radius of the rounded arcs applied to the shape’s edges. Note: hard edges may appear where the rounding radiuses intersect.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_op_shell.description: Makes an SDF shape hollow, with adjustable thickness for its resulting envelope.

thickness.description: The thickness of the shell, applied both inwards and outwards. The shell is rounded when thickness is increased.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_op_subtraction.description: Subtracts the volume of the SDF 1 shape from the SDF 2 shape.

sdf_1.description: The SDF shape being subtracted from.

sdf_2.description: The SDF shape being subtracted from the SDF 1 shape.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_op_subtraction_smooth.description: Subtracts the volume of the SDF 1 shape from the SDF 2 shape, with adjustable smoothing applied at the intersection of the two.

sdf_1.description: The SDF shape being subtracted from.

sdf_2.description: The SDF shape being subtracted from the SDF 1 shape.

smoothness.description: The smoothing applied at the intersection of the two shapes. Note: hard edges may appear where the smoothing radiuses intersect.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_op_symmetry.description: Flips and duplicates an SDF shape across a mirror plane, then returns the union of the base SDF shape and its duplicate(s). Symmetry can be applied on any axes concurrently.

flip_axis.description: Sets which axes should be flipped. E.g., (1, 0, 0) will flip the direction of symmetry on the X axis.

mirror_axis.description: Sets the desired mirror axes. E.g., (1, 0, 0) will apply symmetry on the X axis.

mirror_plane_position.description: The world space position of the center of the mirror plane. This position is shared by all mirror planes if the symmetry is applied on several axes.

pre_offset.description: The offset on the X, Y, Z axes applied to the shape before applying the symmetry operator.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_op_union.description: Returns the added volumes of two SDF shapes.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_op_union_chamfer.description: Returns the added volumes of two SDF shapes, with an additional volume of adjustable radius along the edges of their intersection.

radius.description: The radius of the volume added along the edges of the shapes’ intersection.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_op_union_smooth.description: Returns the added volumes of two SDF shapes, with adjustable smoothing of the edges of their intersection.

smoothness.default: 0 Note: hard edges may appear where the smoothing radiuses intersect
smoothness.description: The smoothing radius, starting from the intersection’s edges.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_plane.description: An SDF function for a plane of adjustable orientation, position and size.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the plane.

normal.description: The world space normal vector of the plane, which controls its orientation. The vector is normalized.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

size.description: The size of the plane in X and Y.

thickness.description: The thickness of the plane, applied in all directions. The plane is rounded when thickness is increased.

3d_sdf_pyramid.description: An SDF function for a pyramid of adjustable height, base size and base position.

base_position.description: The world space position of the pyramid’s base.

base_size.description: The size of the pyramid’s base in X and Y.

height.description: The Z-up height of the pyramid’s apex from its base.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

3d_sdf_pyramid_square.description: An SDF function for a pyramid with a square base, with adjustable height and base position.

base_position.description: The world space position of the pyramid’s base.

base_size.description: The length of the pyramid’s base edges. All edges are of equal length.

height.description: The Z-up height of the pyramid’s apex from its base.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

3d_sdf_rock.description: An SDF function for a parametric and randomizable rock shape, built with SDF functions.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the rock.

max_facets.description: The maximum number of facets of the rock (up to 32).

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

randomness.description: Jitters the faces orientation and distance to the center. As a result, greater values result in a smaller rock.

scale.description: Global scale of the rock shape. Applied after Randomness , and before Smoothness .

smoothness.description: The radius of the rounded arcs applied to the rock’s edges.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the sphere.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_torus.description: An SDF function for a torus, which is a shape formed by sweeping a minor circle along a major circle. Both circles have adjustable radiuses.

center_position.description: The world space position of the pivot of the torus.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

radius_major.description: The radius of the circle along which the minor disc is swept to form the surface of the torus.

radius_minor.description: The radius of the circle being swept along the major circle to form the surface of the torus.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_transform_bend.description: Bends an SDF shape around its local Y axis between a start and end point at an angle. Note: Since this transformation function is inexact, artefacts might appear when rendering it.

angle.description: The angle, in turns, of the rotation applied at the end of the bend.

end.description: The world position on the Z axis where the bending ends. All the volume above is uniformly rotated at the specified angle.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

start.description: The world position on the Z axis where the bending starts. All the volume beneath is not bent.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_transform_elongate.description: Elongate an SDF shape from an adjustable position. Effectively linearly extends the volume of an SDF shape starting at an adjustable slice.

center_position.description: The world space position from which the shape will be elongated. I.e., the position of the slice being elongated.

elongation.description: The elongation length on the X, Y, Z axes.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_transform_flip.description: Applies a mirror transform to the input SDF shape. Essentially performs a negative scale on the selected axes.

mirror_axis.description: Use an Integer3 to set the desired mirror axis. E.g. (1, 0, 0) will mirror the X axis.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_transform_offset.description: Offset an SDF shape along a vector.

offset.description: The distance the SDF shape will be offset in the X, Y, Z directions.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_transform_offset_p.description: Offsets the world space along a vector. The output transformed world position can be connected to the P input of most SDF functions to define them in this transformed world space. Tip: P transforms can be chained, but keep in mind the results depend on the order of operations.

offset.description: The distance the world space will be offset in the X, Y and Z directions.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_transform_rotate.description: Rotate an SDF shape around one or several axes from an adjustable pivot point, in turns. Use the Transform pivot helper of the 3D Viewer to visualize the performed rotation.

angle.description: The angle, in turns, at which the SDF shape is rotated. The angle is visualized by a circle in the Transform pivot helper of the 3D Viewer . Align the camera so as to see the Axis arrow as the center of this circle to clearly see the angle of your rotation as a fraction of a turn.

axis.description: The normalized vector defining the axis around which the SDF shape is rotated. E.g. (0, 1, 0) will rotate the SDF shape around the Y axis of its local pivot point. The axis is visualized by an arrow in the Transform pivot helper of the 3D Viewer . The color of the arrow is mapped on the XYZ components of this vector.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

pivot_position.description: The world space position of the local pivot of the SDF shape, where (0, 0, 0) places the pivot at the center of the SDF shape. Defines the origin of the rotation. The pivot is visualized by the start of the arrow in the Transform pivot helper of the 3D Viewer .

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_transform_rotate_p.description: Rotate the world space around an axis at an adjustable angle. The output transformed world position can be connected to the P input of most SDF functions to define them in this transformed world space. Use the Transform pivot helper of the 3D Viewer to visualize the performed rotation. Tip: P transforms can be chained, but keep in mind the results depend on the order of operations.

angle.description: The angle, in turns, at which the world space is rotated. The angle is visualized by a circle in the Transform pivot helper of the 3D Viewer . Align the camera so as to see the Axis arrow as the center of this circle to clearly see the angle of your rotation as a fraction of a turn.

axis.description: The normalized vector defining the axis around which the world space is rotated. E.g. (0, 1, 0) will rotate the world space around the Y axis of the pivot point. The axis is visualized by an arrow in the Transform pivot helper of the 3D Viewer . The color of the arrow is mapped on the XYZ components of this vector.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

pivot_position.description: The world space position of the pivot defining the origin of the rotation. The pivot point is visualized by the start of the arrow in the Transform pivot helper of the 3D Viewer .

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

pivot_position.description: The world space position of the local pivot of the SDF shape, where (0, 0, 0) places the pivot at the center of the SDF shape. Defines the origin of the scaling.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

3d_sdf_transform_twist.description: Twist an SDF shape around its local Z axis between a start and end point, at an adjustable angle. Note: Since this transformation function is inexact, artefacts might appear when rendering it.

angle.description: The angle, in turns, of the rotation applied at the end of the twist.

end.description: The world position on the Z axis where the twisting ends. All the volume above is uniformly rotated at the specified angle.

p_input.description: The transformed world space position. Use this input to apply additional transformations using the Offset P and Rotate P nodes.

start.description: The world position on the Z axis where the twisting starts. All the volume beneath is not twisted.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

set_color.description:
Set the base color of an SDF scene’s material.

This color can then be retrieved for all splattered SDF shapes in the SDF color output of the Shape splatter v2 .

basecolor.description: The RGB base color value to set.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

set_id.description:
Set the material ID of an SDF scene.

This value can then be retrieved for all splattered SDF shapes in the Splatter UVW output of the Shape splatter v2 .

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

set_material.description:
Set the base color, roughness and metalness of the material of an SDF scene.

These values can then be retrieved for all splattered SDF shapes in the outputs of the Shape splatter v2 .

basecolor.description: The RGB base color value to set.

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

set_metalness.description:
Set the metalness of an SDF scene’s material.

This value can then be retrieved for all splattered SDF shapes in the SDF metalness output of the Shape splatter v2 .

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.

set_roughness.description:
Set the roughness of an SDF scene’s material.

This value can then be retrieved for all splattered SDF shapes in the SDF roughness output of the Shape splatter v2 .

ports_evidence.notes: Inputs use package scan host port ids when available; unique_filter_output is supplied by the static SDF function contract rule.

evidence.notes: SDF library output type is supplied by static contract; root selectability needs live probe confirmation.
